title: It can be easier than I expect
subtitle: An affirmation for interrupting the habit of expecting financial difficulty.
excerpt: A short affirmation for Day 17 of the MONEY program. A single statement that interrupts the default assumption that financial progress requires struggle.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 17 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use at the start of a day when you are approaching a financial task expecting it to be difficult.

content.text: A short affirmation for interrupting the expectation of difficulty. The statement doesn't promise ease — it opens the possibility of it. "Can be" is doing specific work: it bypasses the resistance that comes from overclaiming.
It can be easier than I expect.
Say it three times before approaching a financial task you have been dreading. Let the possibility land without needing proof. If a phrase doesn't feel like you, change it.
Adapted from Day 17 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
